law: Toronto Parks Bylaw, Toronto Municipal Code Ch. 608 (encampment enforcement)
purposes: To regulate use of city parks for public enjoyment and safety

provisions.text: No person shall camp in a park unless authorized by permit. (from training data)
No person shall place, install, attach or erect a temporary or permanent tent or structure of any kind in a park unless authorized by permit. (from training data)

Where an officer has reasonable grounds to believe that a person has contravened any provision of this chapter, the officer may issue a notice of trespass to the person, requiring the person to leave the park immediately. (from training data)

derivation:
MTM-Literal derivation
  m = |purposes| = 2
  n = |provisions| = 3
  purposes served by ≥1 provision = 2/2
  → F (Faithfulness) = 2/2 = 1.0000
  provisions serving no purpose = 0/3
  → X (Excess)       = 0/3 = 0.0000
  α = 0.5, β = 0.5
  MTM-L = 100 · [α · (1 − F) + β · X]
        = 100 · [0.5 · (1 − 1.0000) + 0.5 · 0.0000]
        = 100 · [0.0000 + 0.0000]
        = 0.00
